title: onobrychis oxyodonta
intro: Onobrychis oxyodonta, also known as the sainfoin or holy clover, is a perennial legume plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to increase milk production in lactating animals and its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Onobrychis oxyodonta include its potential to improve digestion, reduce symptoms of menopause, and provide relief from arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. In herbal preparations, Onobrychis oxyodonta is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in dried or powdered forms, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
